title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root

input_row.title: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root
input_row.description: Ensure that the kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root .
input_row.rationale: The kubelet service file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the kubelet service in the worker node. You should set its file ownership to maintain the integrity of the file. The file should be owned by root:root .
input_row.audit: ```Run the following command: 1 Should return root:root for each node for node in $(oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}') do oc debug node/${node} -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service done Verify that the ownership is set to `root:root`.
input_row.remediation: Run the following command: chown root:root /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service
input_row.default_value: By default, kubelet service file ownership is set to root:root .

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Connect to your IBM Cloud Cluster:**
   - Use the IBM Cloud CLI to authenticate and connect to your OpenShift cluster.
   - Command: `ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster-name`
   - This sets the `KUBECONFIG` environment variable to point to the configuration of your cluster.

2. **Identify OpenShift Nodes:**
   - List the nodes in your OpenShift cluster.
   - Command: `oc get nodes -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}'`
   - This retrieves the names of all nodes in the cluster.

3. **Check Kubelet Service File Ownership:**
   - For each node, execute the following command:
   - Command: `oc debug node/<node-name> -- chroot /host stat -c %U:%G /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service`
   - Replace `<node-name>` with each node retrieved in the previous step.

4. **Verify Ownership:**
   - Ensure that the output of the command matches `root:root` for each node.
   - Check the command output: if it outputs anything other than `root:root`, note it for remediation.

5. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - The ownership of the `/etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service` file should be set to `root:root` on all nodes.

6. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - If all nodes return `root:root`, then compliance is confirmed.
   - If not, refer back to the remediation step to change the ownership to `root:root` using the command:
   - Command: `chown root:root /etc/systemd/system/kubelet.service` (execute this on each affected node). 

This comprehensive approach ensures that all aspects of the control are manually verified.
